rationale: The ensures' antecedent H is the IF of an implication, not a body-level hypothesis, so we wrap the whole proof in `if H { ... }` to make those conjuncts available. Inside H: revealing `StrictlyOrderedVec::valid` gives `sorted(p1)` and `sorted(p2)`. By Seq::insert axioms, in the case r1 < r2 we get p1[r1]=k, p1[r1+1]=s[r1] and p2[r1]=s[r1], p2[r2]=k; sortedness then forces k<s[r1] and s[r1]<k under `cmp_spec`. `K::cmp_properties` (asymmetry: lt<==>swapped-gt) collapses these to k.cmp_spec(s[r1]) being both Less and Greater — impossible — so `assert(false)`. The symmetric branch closes the same way, so r1==r2, and Seq congruence lifts to post1_self_@ == post2_self_@.
